discussion_comments.comment_body: This will lose stack trace information. I would recommend reverting your changes to this file, defining error as `let ERROR;`, and then replacing uses of `ERROR` with a `getErrorObject` function that instantiates `ERROR` when it is first called.
On second thought, I'm not convinced the difference is meaningful, so this could go ahead as is. @colinhacks any thoughts?
maybe leave throw outside of the `zodErrorHandler` to not to break error stack trace?
Good point to discuss from one side more clear stack trace from other side code duplication :(

maybe pass `customError` as closure?

```
const zodErrorHandler = createZodErrorHandler(def.customError);
```
I will overthink it do you mean it will allow to clean stacktrace ?

Missing here messages for `too_big` and `too_small`. Without it for `file` the error goes to the generic:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/49499/158582704-42cbfd4e-3cfa-4d97-bfbf-43b59ac1673b.png)
I can do these changes if you give me push permission to your branch. If not is fine I'll wait :)

With a quick scan, can't find anywhere else where `error as Error` is used.
So if you need it to be a `message: string`, check for `instanceof` first, or use the `...errorUtil.errToObj`

IMO `as` is better here than doing something at runtime. `JSON.parse(...)` only throws `Error`s (specifically `SyntaxError`s) as [documented by MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/JSON/parse#exceptions). I also don't feel particularly strongly though.
I'm not sure if there is a way to handle pluralization or not. If not, maybe `élément(s)` would be better. Mainly to avoid incorrect phrasing when the max value is 1.
return; // early return means malformed + too-long addresses only get too_big, never a format error
